metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4718
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2a, S6: Pulldowns with GST-FCHO2 in rat brain lysate identified known Clathrin Coated Pit (CCP) proteins eps15, eps15R and intersectin 1&2 as main interaction partners.

Figure S4: overexpression of FCHO2 in DIV4 rat hippocampal neurons showed co-localization with clathrin at distinct neurite puncta (S4b, low resolution). Clathrin signals within neuronal cell bodies (endosomes, pericentriolar area) remained in FCHo1+2 RNAi (knockdown) neurons, but not the puncta on neurites (S4c). Furthermore, SYT1 surface expression was increased (S4d) and SYP fluorescence was decreased following induced exocytosis (S4e) upon FCHo1+2 knockdown, indicating a decrease of CME and SV recycling.

Authors showed FCHO2 is involved in Clathrin-Mediated Endocytosis and is localized at Clathrin Coated Pits (CCP) in HeLa cells using high-resolution EM and live cell imaging. Next, authors showed FCHO2 interacts with known synaptic CME proteins, co-localizes with CCPs at neurites in rat brain lysate and knockdown affects SV endocytosis. Although imaging resolution is too low to pin-point localization to pre/post-synapse (or both), these results imply FCHO2 is localized at synapses and involved in CME.. Experimental description: Figure 2a: "Pull down with GST-FCHo2-μHD and rat brain lysate"
